pass_summary: All 7 tasks implemented correctly. Async hooks, subagent-scoped MCP, UserPromptSubmit context injection, enriched compaction, and embedded assets are all present and well-structured. Tests cover the new prompt-context builder thoroughly. OpenCode MCP scoping gap is documented as expected.

issues.title: research.md subagent missing background: true
issues.description: targets/claude-code/agents/research.md does not include `background: true` in its YAML frontmatter, unlike plan-reviewer.md and spec-reviewer.md which both have it. This means research runs as a foreground agent, which may be intentional for interactive use but is inconsistent with the other subagents created in Task 3.
issues.suggested_fix: If background execution is desired (consistent with other subagents), add `background: true` to the YAML frontmatter in targets/claude-code/agents/research.md. If foreground is intentional, add a comment in the plan noting the design choice.

issues.title: No integration test for hint() output format with UserPromptSubmit event
issues.description: prompt-context.test.ts tests the buildSpecContext() pure function (good), but doesn't test the main() function's integration with readStdin/hint/output. Specifically, it doesn't verify that hint('UserPromptSubmit', context) produces the correct JSON structure expected by Claude Code's hook runner. This is acceptable since the hint() function is tested elsewhere and the hook is simple, but is a minor coverage gap.
issues.suggested_fix: Optionally add a test that mocks readStdin() and captures stdout to verify the full JSON output structure from main(). Low priority since hint() is a well-tested utility.

truths.truth: After enabling async hooks, tdd-tracker, memory-observer, and context-monitor do not block Claude Code's tool pipeline
truths.evidence: hooks.json diff shows `"async": true` added to tdd-tracker (line 73), memory-observer (line 94), and context-monitor (line 105). All three are PostToolUse hooks that won't block the pipeline.

truths.truth: External MCP tools (context7, web-search, grep-mcp, web-fetch) are NOT available in main conversation but ARE available inside plan-reviewer, spec-reviewer, and research subagents
truths.evidence: .mcp.json diff removes all 4 external servers, keeping only sentinal. plan-reviewer.md, spec-reviewer.md, and new research.md all have mcpServers frontmatter with inline definitions of all 4 external servers.

truths.truth: When an active spec plan exists, every Claude Code prompt submission includes the active plan path, current task, and progress percentage in the context
truths.evidence: hooks.json has new UserPromptSubmit entry pointing to `sentinal hook shared prompt-context`. prompt-context.ts buildSpecContext() outputs plan path, status, progress %, current task, and remaining count. hook.ts dispatch map includes 'prompt-context'. Tests confirm output contains all expected fields.

truths.truth: OpenCode's compaction handler injects enriched spec state (plan path + current task + progress) into post-compaction context
truths.evidence: sentinal.ts compaction handler diff shows enriched context with tasks array parsing, completed/remaining counts, percent calculation, currentTask detection (in-progress then pending fallback), and formatted spec lines pushed to output.context. Cross-reference comment to prompt-context.ts is present.

truths.truth: OpenCode's session.created handler injects memory context into the LLM's context (not just TUI log)
truths.evidence: sentinal.ts session.created handler now writes compact-state.json with memoryContext and activePlan after restoring context via sidecar. The compaction handler reads this file, ensuring memory context reaches the LLM via output.context.push() on first compaction. Comments explain the TUI-only limitation of client.app.log().

Cannot execute tests in review environment (bash restricted to git commands). Test file exists with 8 well-structured test cases covering all edge cases. Code is structurally sound with no visible type errors or runtime issues.

truths.truth: sentinal install correctly writes all updated configs
truths.evidence: embedded-assets.ts regenerated (timestamp 2026-03-17T11:11:21.225Z) with updated hooks.json (async flags, UserPromptSubmit), .mcp.json (sentinal only), agents (mcpServers frontmatter on all 3), and new research.md agent. The installer reads from embedded-assets.ts, so install output will match.
